items.quote: Singing for me is sweet relief. It is the only time when I feel fluent. It is the only time when what comes out of my mouth is comprehensively exactly what I intended.
items.speaker: Megan Washington

items.quote: It's impossible to stutter when you sing.
items.speaker: Megan Washington

items.quote: Scatting gave me a way to stutter freely. I'm a star . . . not although I stutter, but because I stutter.
items.speaker: John Larkin (Scatman John)

items.quote: The fact that I've been a stutterer since I've been speaking has compelled me to find another way to speak another language.
items.speaker: John Larkin (Scatman John)

items.quote: And by learning that record — and rapping it back, to back, to back, to back — it cured my stutter.
items.speaker: Ed Sheeran

items.quote: Embrace your weirdness. From a stuttering point of view, don't treat it as an issue. Work through it and get the treatment you want to get, but don't ever see it as a plight on your life. Carry on pushing forward.
items.speaker: Ed Sheeran
items.publication: HuffPost (reporting on his 2015 American Institute for Stuttering gala speech)

items.quote: I felt so strangulated talking that I did the natural thing, which is to write songs, because I could sing without stammering, as all stammerers can.
items.speaker: Carly Simon

items.quote: I used to stutter. And when I sang, I didn't stutter, and singing became my safe haven.
items.speaker: Marc Anthony

items.quote: As a kid, I used to stutter. I think that's why I put my energy into making music. That's how I get my thoughts out, instead of being crazy all the time.
items.speaker: Kendrick Lamar

items.quote: I lived through my music because I couldn't speak well.
items.speaker: Gareth Gates

items.quote: Words aren't my friends. Music is. Sounds, notes, rhythms. I talk through music.
items.speaker: B.B. King
items.publication: Blues All Around Me: The Autobiography of B.B. King (cited by Stuttering Foundation)
